dashboard.disclaimer: Medical Disclaimer: This application provides general health information only and is not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.
tips: Staying hydrated improves energy levels and brain function. Aim for 8 glasses of water daily.
A 30-minute walk each day reduces the risk of heart disease by up to 35%.
Getting 7–9 hours of sleep each night strengthens your immune system.
Eating 5 servings of fruits and vegetables daily lowers chronic disease risk.

chat.title: RHC AI Health Assistant
chat.disclaimer: ⚕️ This AI does not replace professional medical advice. Always consult a qualified healthcare provider.
chat.emergencyTitle: This may be a medical emergency!
chat.placeholder: Ask a health question… (Enter to send)
chat.welcome:
Hello! I'm **RHC AI**, your Rural Health Companion. 👋

I can help you with:
- **Preventive healthcare** tips
- **Symptom guidance** and when to seek care
- **Nutrition, hydration & rest** advice
- **Hygiene & sanitation** best practices
- **Emergency warning signs**

How can I help you today?

> ⚕️ *This AI does not replace professional medical advice.*

titles.When to Seek Immediate Medical Care: When to Seek Immediate Medical Care
titles.Essential Vitamins: Folic Acid and Iron: Essential Vitamins: Folic Acid and Iron

contents.Seek emergency care if you experience: s: Seek emergency care if you experience: severe bleeding, sudden severe swelling in the face or hands, severe headaches with visual changes, fever over 38°C (100.4°F), or decreased fetal movement.
contents.If you are less than 37 weeks pregnant,: If you are less than 37 weeks pregnant, watch out for: regular contractions (every 10 minutes or more often), leaking fluid from your vagina, pelvic pressure, or low, dull backache.
contents.Folic acid helps prevent neural tube def: Folic acid helps prevent neural tube defects. Take 400mcg daily. Iron is crucial for increased blood volume. Iron-rich foods include spinach, lentils, fortified cereals, and lean meats. Pair iron with Vitamin C for better absorption.
contents.Avoid: raw or undercooked meat, unpasteu: Avoid: raw or undercooked meat, unpasteurized milk and cheese, raw sprouts, high-mercury fish (like shark, swordfish), and excess caffeine (limit to 200mg per day).
contents.Aim for 30 minutes of moderate exercise: Aim for 30 minutes of moderate exercise most days. Safe options include brisk walking, swimming, prenatal yoga, and stationary cycling. Avoid contact sports, activities with a high fall risk, and hot yoga.
contents.Pack your bag by week 36. Include: medic: Pack your bag by week 36. Include: medical records, ID, comfortable clothes, nursing bras, maternity pads, toiletries, clothes for the baby, and diapers.
contents.Your baby's major organs are forming. Yo: Your baby's major organs are forming. You may experience fatigue, nausea, and tender breasts. Focus on resting and taking your prenatal vitamins.
contents.Often the most comfortable trimester. Na: Often the most comfortable trimester. Nausea usually subsides. You will start feeling the baby move (quickening) around weeks 16-20. Your bump will become visible.
contents.Your baby is gaining weight rapidly. You: Your baby is gaining weight rapidly. You may feel more tired, experience backaches, and have Braxton Hicks contractions. Start monitoring baby's movements daily (kick counts).
